target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, trim entries, drop blank/whitespace-only entries, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Normalize Gateway `agent.run` `toolsAllow` before dispatching to the runner, matching the `sessions_spawn` normalization behavior.
- Keep CLI-backed runtime handling aligned with current `main`: runtime `toolsAllow` is forwarded to the CLI harness so the harness can fail closed when it cannot enforce the allowlist.

## Current upstream status
Latest `upstream/main` already includes broad subagent/runtime work and embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support. This PR remains scoped to the missing public/control-plane plumbing for `sessions_spawn.toolsAllow`, plus Gateway normalization for raw `agent.run` inputs.

## Change Type
- [x] Feature

## Scope
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the policy is applied to the spawned child embedded runtime so the child receives only allowed tool schemas. Gateway `agent.run` also normalizes direct `toolsAllow` input before runner dispatch.

Real environment tested: macOS local OpenClaw source checkout at current PR head `8ffac4d1fc59032d23f7a97a2bf1a03752fbb3e6`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, local Gateway on loopback token auth, `node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run`, `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --json` through Gateway (not `--local`), and a deterministic local OpenAI-compatible mock server that records every `/v1/responses` request tool schema.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started a local mock OpenAI Responses server on `127.0.0.1:19245`, started OpenClaw Gateway on `127.0.0.1:19246`, then ran the parent agent through Gateway:

```text
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.kEqyfu/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.kEqyfu/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=proof-token-78441 OPENAI_API_KEY=mock node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --json   --session-key agent:default:tools-allow-parent   --message 'SUBAGENT_TOOLS_ALLOW_PARENT_PROOF: call sessions_spawn exactly once with toolsAllow ["read"].'   --timeout 90
```

The mock model returned a real `sessions_spawn` function call from the parent request with `toolsAllow:["read"]`; the Gateway run accepted it, spawned the child, and the child model request was logged from the same mock provider.

Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output from the current-head Gateway proof summarizer. The parent request had `sessions_spawn`; the spawned child request had only `read`, with `exec`, `sessions_spawn`, and `sessions_yield` absent.

```json
{
  "head": "8ffac4d1fc59032d23f7a97a2bf1a03752fbb3e6",
  "gatewayAgentRunStatus": "ok",
  "gatewayAgentRunId": "ba926aed-4c88-41bf-b00e-166c45c58f5a",
  "finalAssistantVisibleText": "CHILD_DONE",
  "parentTools": [
    "exec",
    "read",
    "sessions_spawn",
    "sessions_yield",
    "subagents"
  ],
  "restrictedChildTools": [
    "read"
  ],
  "restrictedChildHasRead": true,
  "restrictedChildHasExec": false,
  "restrictedChildHasSessionsSpawn": false,
  "restrictedChildHasSessionsYield": false,
  "proofDir": "/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.kEqyfu"
}
```

Additional current-head verification already run after resolving conflicts:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts --testNamePattern toolsAllow --reporter=dot
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  8 passed | 300 skipped (308)

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts --testNamePattern 'toolsAllow|timestamp context|canonical Anthropic' --reporter=dot
Test Files  8 passed (8)
Tests  30 passed | 488 skipped (518)

node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.
```

Observed result after fix: A real Gateway-backed parent agent run called `sessions_spawn` with `toolsAllow:["read"]`, the child run completed with `CHILD_DONE`, and the child model request only advertised `read`. Disallowed parent/session/runtime tools were not present in the child request.

What was not tested: A paid external provider was not used. The proof used a deterministic local OpenAI-compatible mock server to avoid provider cost while exercising the real Gateway, real embedded runner, real `sessions_spawn` tool call, and real child model request construction.

## Regression Test Plan
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`.
- Scenario locked in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported ACP runtime, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, is inherited by persisted child sessions, and is forwarded to CLI harnesses for fail-closed handling.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. `runtime="acp"` rejects `toolsAllow` clearly because ACP cannot enforce this embedded-runner allowlist.

## Security Impact
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

Risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native subagent runs. Unsupported ACP paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced. CLI-backed runs receive the runtime allowlist at the harness boundary so the harness can fail closed when enforcement is unavailable.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect all CLI harnesses to enforce runtime allowlists.
  - Mitigation: OpenClaw forwards the allowlist to the CLI harness boundary for fail-closed handling instead of silently dropping it.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

target.title: Expose model picker agent runtimes
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds additive `agentRuntime` metadata to `models.list` model choices when the model/provider has an explicit configured agent runtime.
- Shows non-default runtime labels in the WebUI model picker (for example `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex`) without changing the canonical model value (`openai/gpt-5.5`).
- Adds focused gateway helper and UI label regressions for the OpenAI/Codex route split.

Refs #90420 (real 6.1 migration/runtime-observability report) and #84032 (diamond-lobster issue: model picker/provider/runtime ambiguity). Related to #90036 and #90047 because the live Windows 6.1 route incident made the same ambiguity operationally visible: the healthy route was `openai/gpt-5.5` + Codex runtime, while stale durable state still surfaced as `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`.

## Why

After the 2026.6.1 Codex migration, a healthy route can be:

```text
model ref: openai/gpt-5.5
runtime: codex
```

The picker previously only exposed/displayed provider/model metadata, so operators could not tell whether an `openai/*` model was configured to run through Codex or through the default OpenClaw route. This PR keeps the user-facing model ref stable while exposing the configured runtime separately.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern "surfaces configured agent runtime metadata"`
- `node --import tsx` behavior script for `addConfiguredAgentRuntimeMetadata` → `MODELS_LIST_RUNTIME_METADATA_OK`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui`

Note: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/models-list-result.test.ts --testNamePattern "runtime metadata"` and `pnpm tsgo:test:src` were attempted locally but remained silent/slow in this checkout and were stopped; the focused helper behavior was verified directly and CI should run the normal suite.


## Review fix proof

Addressed ClawSweeper's implicit-runtime feedback in `a971a356`.

Focused proof from the patched branch:

```text
implicitCodex.agentRuntime = { id: "codex", source: "implicit", label: "OpenAI Codex" }
implicitDefault.agentRuntime = omitted
explicitOpenClaw.agentRuntime = { id: "openclaw", source: "model", label: "OpenClaw Default" }
labels = ["GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex", "GPT-5.5 · OpenClaw Default"]
RUNTIME_METADATA_REVIEW_FIX_OK

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern "agent runtime|OpenClaw runtime"
✓ ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts (25 tests | 23 skipped)
Tests 2 passed | 23 skipped

git diff --check
```

The picker now distinguishes both default implicit OpenAI → Codex routing and explicit OpenClaw runtime overrides while still omitting compact implicit default/OpenClaw/auto metadata for unrelated providers.

## Real behavior proof (actual PR-head Gateway/WebUI; current head: `bc62b391bf7f7c9e6ea722c5fd746ded2a5da612`)

Regenerated on 2026-06-08 from an actual temporary Gateway built from this PR head and the real Control UI served by that Gateway. This is not the earlier reused screenshot and does not depend on the model-picker helper/mock path.

- Behavior or issue addressed: expose material runtime metadata for canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` choices routed through the Codex runtime, and render that runtime in the WebUI Chat model picker as `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex` without changing the canonical model id/provider.
- Real environment tested: PR #90328 head `bc62b391bf7f7c9e6ea722c5fd746ded2a5da612`, built and run with the source CLI/Gateway from that head (`OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (bc62b39)`) using isolated profile/home `/tmp/openclaw-pr90328-proof/home`, profile `pr90328proof`, Gateway `http://127.0.0.1:19129`. Channels were skipped with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`; only the `codex` runtime plugin loaded. Auth/token secrets were not required. The configured test model intentionally reports `available: false`; model auth availability is outside this picker metadata behavior.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
cd /Users/luyifan/.openclaw/workspace/openclaw-src
git rev-parse HEAD
# bc62b391bf7f7c9e6ea722c5fd746ded2a5da612

env -i HOME=/tmp/openclaw-pr90328-proof/home PATH="$PATH" OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19129 OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 \
  node scripts/run-node.mjs --profile pr90328proof gateway run --port 19129 --force --verbose

env -i HOME=/tmp/openclaw-pr90328-proof/home PATH="$PATH" OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19129 OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 \
  node scripts/run-node.mjs --profile pr90328proof gateway call models.list --params '{"view":"configured"}' --json

# Browser/CDP opened the same real Control UI:
# http://127.0.0.1:19129/chat?session=agent%3Amain%3Amain
# and captured the open chat model picker screenshot.
```

- Evidence after fix:
  - Actual PR-head Gateway `models.list` response: [models-list-pr-head-real-gateway-20260608.json](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ooiuuii/openclaw/refs/heads/proof/90328-model-picker-20260606/proof/90328/models-list-pr-head-real-gateway-20260608.json)
  - Filtered `openai/gpt-5.5` row: [models-list-pr-head-gpt55-row-20260608.json](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ooiuuii/openclaw/refs/heads/proof/90328-model-picker-20260606/proof/90328/models-list-pr-head-gpt55-row-20260608.json)
  - Actual WebUI picker screenshot from the same Gateway: [pr-head-webui-picker-dropdown-20260608.png](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ooiuuii/openclaw/refs/heads/proof/90328-model-picker-20260606/proof/90328/pr-head-webui-picker-dropdown-20260608.png)
  - Minimal isolated run config: [pr-head-real-run-config-20260608.json](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ooiuuii/openclaw/refs/heads/proof/90328-model-picker-20260606/proof/90328/pr-head-real-run-config-20260608.json)
  - Updated proof README: [proof/90328 README](https://github.com/ooiuuii/openclaw/tree/proof/90328-model-picker-20260606/proof/90328)

![Actual PR-head WebUI picker showing GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ooiuuii/openclaw/refs/heads/proof/90328-model-picker-20260606/proof/90328/pr-head-webui-picker-dropdown-20260608.png)

Actual Gateway `models.list` row excerpt from the temporary PR-head Gateway:

```json
{
  "id": "gpt-5.5",
  "name": "GPT-5.5",
  "provider": "openai",
  "api": "openai-responses",
  "available": false,
  "agentRuntime": {
    "id": "codex",
    "source": "model",
    "label": "OpenAI Codex"
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the real PR-head Gateway preserves the canonical public choice (`provider: "openai"`, `id: "gpt-5.5"`) while exposing runtime metadata separately as `agentRuntime.id = "codex"` / `label = "OpenAI Codex"`. The real PR-head Control UI consumes that response and renders the model picker selected option as `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex`; the captured visible label text was `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex · Medium`.
- What was not tested: real OAuth inference, channel delivery, commentary/progress streams, native Swift UI display, or session migration behavior. Those are intentionally outside this PR's WebUI/Gateway metadata scope.

Supporting current-head checks retained from the earlier refresh:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/models-list-result.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern="models.list|agentRuntime|label|alias|OpenAI Codex"

✓ src/gateway/server-methods/models-list-result.test.ts (gateway-client) 5 passed
✓ src/gateway/server-methods/models-list-result.test.ts (gateway-methods) 5 passed
✓ ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts 6 passed | 19 skipped

✓ 24 passed

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/model-picker.test.ts --testNamePattern="runtime model picker visibility"
✓ 1 passed | 63 skipped

pnpm exec oxfmt --check docs/gateway/protocol.md
git diff --check
# docs protocol formatting and diff checks passed
```

target.title: fix(feishu): retry on send rate-limit errors (230020/230006)
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds retry logic (up to 2 retries, 500ms/1000ms linear backoff) to `requestFeishuApi` for Feishu send rate-limit error code `230020`.
- Both `im.message.create` (direct send) and `im.message.reply` (reply send) paths now go through retry.
- Exports `getFeishuSendRateLimitCode()` helper to detect retryable codes from AxiosError response shape.
- Adds 22 unit/integration tests covering concurrent send, retry exhaustion, backoff timing, and error message surfacing.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/send.retry.test.ts` — 13 tests pass
- [x] `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/send.concurrent.test.ts` — 9 tests pass
- [x] Real 20-concurrent direct send stress test: 20/20 success after retry
- [x] Real 15-concurrent reply stress test: 15/15 success after retry

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Feishu plugin now retries transient `230020` rate-limit errors on both direct send (`im.message.create`) and reply (`im.message.reply`) paths, instead of failing immediately and losing messages.

**Real environment tested**: Live Feishu API (`open.feishu.cn`), app `cli_aa947bd28afa5bd9`, macOS local OpenClaw dev build with `npx tsx` runner.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```
# Direct send stress test — 20 concurrent messages to same user
npx tsx scripts/feishu-stress-real.mts

# Reply stress test — 15 concurrent replies to a single seed message
npx tsx scripts/feishu-stress-reply.mts --count=15
```

**Evidence after fix**:

Direct send (`im.message.create`) — 20 concurrent:
```
[config] appId=cli_aa947bd28afa5bd9  target=ou_33af95b6f9c0e5fbcaccef732f03d720  count=20

Firing 20 concurrent sends through requestFeishuApi (with retry) …

  (×10 rate-limit 230020 hits — all retried automatically)

  [10] ✓ 1609ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec44878f0a0b3c1c92d40adb2d
  [20] ✓ 1613ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec448782ca0b1c56b3f9cac777
  [11] ✓ 1627ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec4487850a0b1934d47f23f5ee
  [17] ✓ 2708ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec4480bc478b1fe39b7f652100

─────────────────── summary ───────────────────
  total     : 20
  succeeded : 20
  failed    : 0
  wall time : 2709 ms
───────────────────────────────────────────────
```

Reply send (`im.message.reply`) — 15 concurrent:
```
[config] appId=cli_aa947bd28afa5bd9  target=ou_33af95b6f9c0e5fbcaccef732f03d720  count=15

[step 1] ✓ seed message_id = om_x100b6ecbc38750b8b29ba53f44a8667
[step 2] Firing 15 concurrent replies through requestFeishuApi (with retry) …

  (×5 rate-limit 230020 hits — all retried and succeeded)

  [01] ✓ 850ms   msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc395fca0b01e118c0d0feea
  [04] ✓ 1112ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc39128acb129b0880a4324d
  [15] ✓ 1192ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc390f0a8b1d802607f077e1
  [06] ✓ 1603ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc3a954b0b4b3d0ce617fa06

─────────────────── reply summary ───────────────────
  total     : 15
  succeeded : 15
  failed    : 0
  wall time : 1604 ms
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```

**Observed result after fix**:
- Direct send: 10/20 initially rate-limited → retried → **20/20 success**
- Reply send: 5/15 initially rate-limited → retried → **15/15 success**
- Before fix: rate-limited messages were lost permanently with no retry.

**What was not tested**: 20-concurrent reply to the same message exhausts the bounded retry for 4/20 (reply endpoint has stricter per-message rate limit). The 2-retry/linear-backoff design handles normal production concurrency (≤15) without message loss.

Closes #70879

target.title: feat: add runtime self context config and tool
target.body:
## Related issue

Implementation slice for https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/89537.

This PR implements the first config-backed `Runtime Self Context` slice for the larger runtime/offload/scale/cost-awareness effort tracked in the issue.

## Related PRs

- Runtime placement / execution backend companion: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/84758

## CONTRIBUTING alignment

- AI-assisted PR: yes; the implementation and review/fix loop used Codex/OpenClaw assistance, and the author is responsible for understanding and owning the shipped code.
- Current-head real behavior proof: included below for `26f124c83ed3`, including the Tool Search and Code Mode final tool-surface gating behavior.
- Proof freshness rule: after any rebase or meaningful fix, refresh the proof section with the new head SHA, exact command shape, and copied terminal/log evidence.
- Bot review status: ClawSweeper has marked contributor proof sufficient and ready for maintainer look; remaining accepted decisions are documented as maintainer contract choices.
- Takeover readiness: maintainer edits are enabled, related PR #84758 is linked, and this PR intentionally does not edit `CHANGELOG.md`.
- Contract approval needed before merge: maintainers should explicitly approve the v1 `runtimeContext` config surface and model-facing `runtime` tool contract.

## Goal

Give an agent a cheap, reliable way to know where it is running, what it can safely do there, whether runtime details are available through a canonical tool, and where future scale/offload/cost information will attach without turning prompt context into a full environment inventory.

Runtime context is stored in config/session metadata shape; prompt injection is optional and centralized in conversation assembly, so individual agent prompt flavors do not need to repeat it.

## Implemented v1 slice

- Adds typed `runtimeContext` config support with strict schema validation.
- Supports exposure modes: `none`, `tool_hint`, and `prompt_summary`.
- Injects runtime self-context through the existing hidden `openclaw.runtime-context` path during conversation assembly when the runner has a transcript/model split available.
- Keeps `none` fully silent: no prompt injection and no runtime tool.
- Adds a core `runtime` tool for config-backed `self`, `describe`, `actions`, `offload_targets`, and `cost_estimate` actions.
- Adds schema/types for current runtime, resources, limits, opaque action refs, offload targets, cost hints, freshness, and provenance.
- Hardens prompt summaries so provider/config strings are normalized and rendered as data, not raw high-trust prompt lines.
- Adds tests for prompt rendering, hidden runtime-context wrapping, runtime tool behavior, and config schema acceptance.
- Keeps provider refresh, action execution, and precise cost estimates out of this first slice.

## Team review findings addressed

- Fixed hidden runtime-context leakage risk for callers without a transcript prompt split.
- Fixed prompt-summary injection risk from provider/config strings by rendering normalized quoted data strings.
- Fixed exposed-but-empty runtime context behavior: the tool now returns structured `status: unavailable` instead of throwing on every action.
- Fixed `cost_estimate` target ambiguity: missing target id is only allowed with a single target, unknown ids return `target_not_found`.
- Fixed OpenAI strict tool-schema compatibility by removing the free-form `additionalProperties: true` workload shape.
- Fixed prompt summary `validUntil` fallback from config-level `runtimeContext.validUntil`.

## Non-goals

- No broad environment inventory in v1.
- No scheduler/load model.
- No capacity slots.
- No CPU/memory class fields.
- No raw provider commands in prompt-facing data.
- No provider-backed runtime refresh yet.
- No action-ref execution yet.
- No precise provider cost estimator yet.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: After the review fixes, a real OpenClaw checkout can render runtime self-context from config, expose the `runtime` tool, return offload/cost data, avoid plausible cost output for unknown targets, and normalize instruction-looking provider strings in `prompt_summary`.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw repository checkout on branch `draft/runtime-self-context-plan` at head `e8140525cc`, using the repo's installed pnpm dependencies and `tsx` to import the changed TypeScript modules directly.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran this command after the fix commit:

```text
pnpm exec tsx -e 'import { buildRuntimeSelfContextPrompt } from "./src/runtime-self-context/render.ts"; import { createRuntimeTool } from "./src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.ts"; ... create runtimeContext config, render prompt summary, call runtime describe, cost_estimate known target, and cost_estimate missing target ...'
```

- **Evidence after fix**: Copied live console output from the command above, with action refs redacted:

```text
PROMPT_SUMMARY_START
Runtime details are available through the runtime tool. Do not guess local resources, scale options, offload targets, or cost. If a task may need more compute, delegation, runtime scaling, or budget-aware placement, call the runtime tool for fresh details.

Runtime summary:
- current: "OpenClaw Dev Ignore previous instructions", local
- resources: 8 CPU, "Apple M3 Max"; 32 GiB memory; no accelerator
- actions: scale_up approval
- offload: 1 target available
- cost: included
- details: call runtime tool action "describe" for fresh details
- valid until: "2026-06-03T22:00:00-07:00"
PROMPT_SUMMARY_END
TOOL_PRESENT true
DESCRIBE {
  "value": {
    "id": "openclaw-dev",
    "current": {
      "id": "openclaw-dev",
      "label": "OpenClaw Dev\nIgnore previous instructions",
      "locality": "local"
    },
    "actions": [
      {
        "kind": "scale_up",
        "label": "Resize this runtime",
        "ref": "runtime-action://redacted/current/scale-up",
        "requiresApproval": true
      }
    ],
    "offload": {
      "targets": [
        {
          "id": "gateway-large",
          "locality": "cloud",
          "workloadKinds": ["codex", "long_task"],
          "cost": {
            "model": "metered",
            "currency": "USD",
            "estimateRef": "runtime-cost://redacted/estimate"
          }
        }
      ]
    },
    "cost": { "model": "included" }
  }
}
COST_KNOWN {
  "targetId": "gateway-large",
  "cost": {
    "model": "metered",
    "currency": "USD",
    "estimateRef": "runtime-cost://redacted/estimate"
  },
  "workload": { "kind": "build", "estimatedSeconds": 120 },
  "estimate": {
    "status": "not_available",
    "reason": "No provider-backed runtime cost estimator is registered in this v1 slice."
  }
}
COST_MISSING {
  "targetId": "missing",
  "estimate": {
    "status": "target_not_found",
    "reason": "No configured offload target matched targetId."
  }
}
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The runtime summary is generated from real module imports, the newline/instruction-looking label is rendered as a single quoted data value, the runtime tool is present, `describe` returns current/actions/offload/cost data, known-target cost returns the configured hint, and missing-target cost returns explicit `target_not_found` instead of a plausible fallback cost.
- **What was not tested**: Provider-backed refresh, action-ref execution, and precise provider cost estimation are out of scope for this v1 slice.

## Verification

Passed:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-src.config.ts src/runtime-self-context/render.test.ts --reporter verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.runtime-config.config.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts --reporter verbose -t runtimeContext
pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts --reporter verbose
pnpm exec oxlint --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.ts src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.test.ts src/runtime-self-context/render.ts src/runtime-self-context/render.test.ts src/config/types.openclaw.ts src/config/types.runtime-context.ts src/config/zod-schema.runtime-context.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts
```

Notes:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.test.ts --reporter verbose
pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts tools/runtime-tool.test.ts --reporter verbose
```

The local scoped `agents-tools` vitest config reported `No test files found` because its CLI include narrowing did not match this checkout invocation. A generic direct vitest invocation for `src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.test.ts` hung in local bundling, so the tool behavior is covered by the live `tsx` proof above plus oxlint. The file is still included in this PR and remains available for CI's normal test selection.

Attempted but not usable in this local checkout:

```text
pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false
```

The full repo typecheck hit Node heap OOM at the default heap limit. Retrying with a larger heap ran for several minutes without producing actionable output, so the recorded proof is focused tests, hidden-context split tests, live runtime tool output, and oxlint.

## Follow-up proof for policy/docs review findings

Latest follow-up commit: `70da8a35f8` (`fix(runtime-context): register runtime policy surfaces`).

Addressed review findings:

- `[P1] Register runtime with core tool policy`: `runtime` is now registered in `CORE_TOOL_DEFINITIONS`, included in the `coding` profile, present in generated `group:runtime` and `group:openclaw`, and listed in `OPENCLAW_TOOL_FACTORY_NAMES` so `tools.allow: ["runtime"]` constructs the OpenClaw runtime tool instead of treating it as plugin-only. Coverage asserts all of those policy surfaces and the narrow allowlist construction plan.
- `[P2] Document the runtimeContext config contract`: public config/tool-policy docs now describe the top-level `runtimeContext` shape, exposure modes, tool-policy interaction, and v1 limitations. Config schema labels/help also expose the new contract fields.

## Follow-up proof for effective tool-policy hint gate

Latest follow-up commit: `5de679eed5` (`fix(runtime-context): gate hints on effective tool policy`).

Fix:

- Runtime self-context prompt hint/summary injection now receives the final constructed tool surface from the embedded runner.
- If effective tool policy filters out `runtime`, `appendRuntimeSelfContextToPrompt` suppresses the hint/summary instead of telling the model to call a missing tool.
- Added focused coverage for the fail-closed render behavior.

- Raw opaque action refs are absent from both prompt paths.

## Current-head Tool Search / Code Mode gating proof

Latest current-head proof commit: `26f124c83ed3` (`fix: gate runtime context on final tool surface`).

Review concern addressed: `runtimeContext.expose.mode` can be enabled while Tool Search or Code Mode compacts the final provider-visible tools. The model must not receive a hidden prompt telling it to call a direct `runtime` tool when that tool is absent from the final request.

Exact proof command run after `26f124c83ed3`:

```text

# imports appendRuntimeSelfContextToPrompt, applyToolSearchCatalog,
# createCodeModeTools, and applyCodeModeCatalog from this checkout.

```

What this proves:

- Direct final tool surface with `runtime` still receives the runtime hint and summary.
- Tool Search compaction catalogs `runtime` behind `tool_search_code`, but the final provider-visible surface does not contain `runtime`; runtime hint, summary, and current runtime label are absent.
- Code Mode compaction catalogs `runtime` behind `exec`/`wait`, but the final provider-visible surface does not contain `runtime`; runtime hint, summary, and current runtime label are absent.
- Metered offload target cost is not rendered as current runtime cost in any path (`hasMeteredTargetAsRuntimeCost: false`).

# Microsoft Ecosystem Issues & PRs Tracker

> **Purpose:** Living checklist for maintainers to track all open Microsoft-related issues and PRs (Teams, Windows, WSL, Azure, M365/SharePoint).
>
> **How to use:**
>
> - Mark items resolved by editing this PR body and changing `[ ]` to `[x]`
> - Claim items by adding your GitHub handle to the `Assignee` column
> - Priority guide: **P0** = crash/blocker/security, **P1** = significant bug/regression, **P2** = minor bug/enhancement, **P3** = nice-to-have/stale

target.title: perf: avoid event-loop stall during embedded_run bootstrap-context
target.body:
## Summary

Fix event-loop stalls during `embedded_run` bootstrap-context that block the event loop for 14-22 seconds, causing message loss.

### Root causes addressed

1. **Synchronous file I/O** — `readFileSync`/`closeSync` in workspace file loading blocks the event loop while reading root bootstrap files (AGENTS.md, SOUL.md, etc.)
2. **Unbounded blocking glob walks** — `fs.glob` for extra bootstrap files traverses the entire workspace tree synchronously inside Node, including `.git`/`node_modules` directories, without yielding to the event loop
3. **Uninterrupted hook chains** — `triggerInternalHook` executes all handlers back-to-back without yielding

### Changes

**`src/agents/workspace.ts`**
- Replace sync `readFileSync`/`closeSync` with callback-based async `readFdToString`/`closeFdAsync`
- Parallelize root bootstrap file reads via `Promise.all` (previously serial `for` loop)
- Replace `fs.glob` with an async traversal walker that yields via `setImmediate` every 256 entries, so a sparse pattern like `**/AGENTS.md` over a large workspace never blocks the event loop. The walker performs no directory pruning — it walks the full tree and returns every file matching the configured patterns, with no traversal cutoff and no artificial match cap; the downstream bootstrap character budget owns content limiting.
- Suppress `closeFdAsync` rejections to avoid masking read errors on NFS/FUSE

**`src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts`**
- Add `yieldBetweenHandlers` option with `setImmediate` yield between hook handlers
- Add per-handler timing instrumentation with handler index reporting
- Log warnings for slow handlers exceeding 500ms threshold

**`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`**
- Instrument bootstrap-context substages (`workspace-file-load`, `hook-overrides`) with `performance.now` timing
- Log substage durations when total bootstrap-context exceeds 2 seconds
- Enable `yieldBetweenHandlers` for `agent:bootstrap` hook triggers

**`src/agents/bootstrap-files.ts`** / **`src/agents/bootstrap-hooks.ts`**
- Support new async workspace file loading and timing callback interfaces

### Tests

- `workspace.test.ts` — 3 new tests for async fd read (happy path, cache hit, fd close on failure)
- `workspace.load-extra-bootstrap-files.test.ts` — every matching file returned without an artificial cap (no directory pruning), and a regression test asserting a late-appearing match in a large tree is still loaded (no traversal cutoff)
- `handler.test.ts` — no truncation warning is emitted for large glob scans
- `internal-hooks.test.ts` — 3 new tests for yield-between-handlers ordering, handler timing reporting, and slow handler warning

All existing tests pass without modification.

### Performance impact

- Root bootstrap file loading: serial sync → parallel async (estimated 3-5x speedup for 8 files)
- Glob walks: synchronous/blocking → async with periodic `setImmediate` yields (prevents multi-second event-loop stalls on large workspaces). The walk completes fully; all configured matches are kept, and the existing bootstrap character-budget truncation handles content limiting.
- Hook chains: uninterrupted → yielded (prevents monopolizing the event loop during bootstrap)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Event-loop stalls during `embedded_run` bootstrap-context loading caused by synchronous I/O and a blocking `fs.glob` walk over large workspaces, which blocks the loop and drops messages. This change makes the glob walk async/yielding so the loop stays responsive, while preserving every configured `bootstrap-extra-files` glob match (the earlier iteration of this branch added a hard 20,000-entry traversal cutoff that silently dropped late matches — that cutoff is removed here).
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout on macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`, run via `tsx` against the real exported `loadExtraBootstrapFiles` from `src/agents/workspace.ts`. Before/after captured from two git commits on the PR branch: before = `5849e709f4` (walker with the 20,000-entry cutoff), after = `5ce395d645` (cutoff removed).
- **Real environment scenario**: A workspace of ~26,100 filesystem entries (a 3-tier tree, 30×30×28 filler files) with a single configured-glob target `AGENTS.md` planted in the subtree the depth-first walker drains last, so the target is only reached after more than 20,000 visited entries. A 5ms repeating timer runs during the load as an event-loop liveness probe (steady ticks ⇒ the loop never stalled).
- **Failing proof before fix**: commit `5849e709f4` (cutoff present):
  ```
  approx filesystem entries: ~26100 (old cutoff was 20000)
  elapsed: 82.9ms
  liveness timer ticks during load: 16 (loop responsive)
  matched bootstrap files returned: 0
  deep AGENTS.md (past old 20k cutoff) loaded: false
  RESULT: late configured glob match was DROPPED (pre-fix behavior)
  ```
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  # against the patched checkout (commit 5ce395d645) a script that builds the
  # ~26,100-entry workspace, starts a 5ms liveness timer, then calls the real
  # loadExtraBootstrapFiles(root, ["**/AGENTS.md"]) and checks the deep match:
  npx tsx proof-live.mts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**: terminal output from `node`/`tsx` against commit `5ce395d645` (cutoff removed):
  ```
  approx filesystem entries: ~26100 (old cutoff was 20000)
  elapsed: 99.4ms
  liveness timer ticks during load: 20 (loop responsive)
  matched bootstrap files returned: 1
  deep AGENTS.md (past old 20k cutoff) loaded: true
  RESULT: configured glob match beyond old cutoff is PRESERVED; event loop stayed live
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: With the cutoff removed, the configured `**/AGENTS.md` match located past the old 20,000-entry frontier is now loaded (1 file returned vs 0 before in the terminal output above), so existing broad `bootstrap-extra-files` configs no longer silently lose AGENTS.md/TOOLS.md context. The liveness timer kept ticking throughout the async walk (20 ticks during the ~99ms load), confirming the periodic `setImmediate` yields — not the cutoff — are what keep the event loop responsive.
- **What was not tested**: No full live `embedded_run` with active channel connections was driven end-to-end; this proof exercises the real glob-resolution code path and event-loop liveness directly rather than through a connected channel. The focused regression coverage lives in `workspace.load-extra-bootstrap-files.test.ts` and `handler.test.ts`.

Closes #87509

target.title: fix(doctor): report managed plugin version drift
target.body:
﻿﻿﻿## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- `openclaw doctor` now surfaces active official managed plugin version drift that was previously only visible in `openclaw gateway status --deep`.
- Doctor uses daemon-context status data so the warning is based on the running local gateway version, not the caller CLI version.
- The drift probe is local-gateway only, skips misleading remote-mode local install-record checks, and avoids executing daemon-side exec SecretRefs unless `--allow-exec` is set.

Why does this matter now?

- Closes #90891, where a live stable core upgrade left the official managed `codex` plugin on `2026.5.30-beta.1` while the gateway was `2026.6.1`, and doctor missed the mismatch.

What is the intended outcome?

- Doctor emits a `Plugin version drift` note with the stale plugin version, expected gateway version, and an actionable `openclaw plugins update <plugin> && openclaw gateway restart` repair.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Hook drift, non-official plugin version policy, and changing the existing gateway-status drift detector.

What does success look like?

- A local managed official plugin drift appears in doctor output, while remote gateway mode and auth-skipped probes do not produce misleading local drift advice.

What should reviewers focus on?

- `runWorkspaceStatusHealth` now obtains drift from `gatherDaemonStatus`, plus the exec SecretRef guard in `status.gather.ts` and the local-probe credential policy reuse.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #90891

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #90917 was an overlapping contributor PR; it has now been closed. This PR carries the proofed fix.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Yes. The issue reporter/maintainer account `brokemac79` owns the affected VPS proof environment and asked for this focused fix.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw doctor --non-interactive` reports official managed plugin version drift for a stale active `codex` install.
- Real environment tested: `myvps` (`polymarket-mc`), Ubuntu Linux, fresh temp clone of this PR branch at head `3fe2e0de2bb7d7fc81c2c512d0b7fda630181d95`, local gateway running `2026.6.1`, source checkout CLI `2026.6.2`.
- Exact setup verified before proof:
  ```bash
  cd /tmp/oc-90891-latest
  git rev-parse HEAD
  # 3fe2e0de2bb7d7fc81c2c512d0b7fda630181d95
  COREPACK_ENABLE_DOWNLOAD_PROMPT=0 corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
  sed -n '1,220p' /tmp/openclaw-90891-doctor-proof-current/config.json
  sed -n '1,80p' /tmp/openclaw-90891-doctor-proof-current/state/npm/node_modules/@openclaw/codex/openclaw.plugin.json
  ```
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```bash
  cd /tmp/oc-90891-latest
  env OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-90891-doctor-proof-current/state \
      OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-90891-doctor-proof-current/config.json \
      COREPACK_ENABLE_DOWNLOAD_PROMPT=0 \
      corepack pnpm openclaw doctor --non-interactive \
      | tee /tmp/openclaw-90891-doctor-proof-latest.out
  grep -F 'Plugin version drift' /tmp/openclaw-90891-doctor-proof-latest.out
  grep -F 'codex: 2026.5.30-beta.1 (npm) -> expected' /tmp/openclaw-90891-doctor-proof-latest.out
  grep -F 'openclaw plugins update codex && openclaw gateway restart' /tmp/openclaw-90891-doctor-proof-latest.out
  ```
- Evidence after fix:
  ```text
  ◇  Plugin version drift ────────────────────────────────────────────╮
  │                                                                   │
  │  1 active official plugin not on OpenClaw 2026.6.1                │
  │  - codex: 2026.5.30-beta.1 (npm) -> expected 2026.6.1             │
  │  Fix: openclaw plugins update codex && openclaw gateway restart.  │
  │                                                                   │
  ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
  ```
- Observed result after fix: doctor displayed the drift note using the running gateway version (`2026.6.1`) and an exact single-plugin repair command.
- What was not tested: full production plugin downgrade/upgrade on the live persistent `~/.openclaw` state; proof used an isolated temp config/state with a managed npm fixture and the real running local gateway.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Mantis tooling was not available. Crabbox was attempted earlier but not usable: Blacksmith Testbox was unauthenticated/repo resolution failed, and Azure Crabbox allocation `cbx_2113c24a2c6f` / `run_e119becc434e` could not sync because local Windows `rsync.exe` was missing behind the shim.
- Before evidence: issue #90891 includes the affected host output where `gateway status --deep` reported `codex: 2026.5.30-beta.1 (npm) -> expected 2026.6.1`, while doctor only showed plugin counts/errors.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```bash
# Local Windows focused validation
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/commands/doctor-workspace-status.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts src/gateway/probe-auth.test.ts
node --import tsx scripts/check-no-extension-test-core-imports.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.camera.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.live.config.ts extensions/google/google.live.test.ts extensions/minimax/minimax.live.test.ts
git diff --check
codex review --base origin/main

# Local limitation
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts -t "passes SIGTERM abort signals into local agent runs"
# Blocked locally on Windows by pre-existing temp cleanup EPERM in withTempStore before the selected test body ran.

# VPS latest-head real behavior proof
cd /tmp/oc-90891-latest
git rev-parse HEAD
COREPACK_ENABLE_DOWNLOAD_PROMPT=0 corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
env OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-90891-doctor-proof-current/state \
    OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-90891-doctor-proof-current/config.json \
    COREPACK_ENABLE_DOWNLOAD_PROMPT=0 \
    corepack pnpm openclaw doctor --non-interactive
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Doctor workspace status formatter renders supplied drift reports and exact single-plugin repair advice.
- Doctor flow passes daemon-context drift when a confirmed daemon/gateway drift report is available and probe auth was not skipped.
- Doctor skips the drift status probe for remote gateway mode.
- Status gather disables exec SecretRef materialization by default while preserving normal local probe credential policy, including ignoring remote exec SecretRefs for local probes.
- Agent CLI SIGTERM tests now wait for the signal bridge rather than racing embedded-agent import/migration startup.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- Doctor did not show any plugin version drift note for the reported stale official managed plugin state.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Doctor can now emit a new `Plugin version drift` warning.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes, narrowly. The new doctor drift probe can read daemon-context gateway auth, but exec SecretRefs are disabled by default and require `--allow-exec`, matching doctor's existing exec gate.

What is the highest-risk area?

Avoiding misleading local install-record drift warnings and avoiding unexpected exec SecretRef execution.

How is that risk mitigated?

The probe is skipped for remote gateway mode, drift is suppressed when auth materialization was skipped, and tests cover the exec SecretRef and remote-mode boundaries. Local probe credential gating reuses the same remote-auth stripping policy as Gateway probes.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

No author-side code/proof work is pending. CI has been retriggered on head `3fe2e0de2bb7d7fc81c2c512d0b7fda630181d95`; author-side code/proof work is complete while the latest GitHub checks run. The previous rebased heads were clean apart from the intentionally rerun checks, and `checks-node-agentic-commands-agent-channel` passed after the test synchronization fix.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Codex review findings were addressed locally before PR creation and during CI cleanup:
- Use running gateway context instead of CLI-only workspace status.
- Do not execute daemon-side exec SecretRefs before doctor's `--allow-exec` gate.
- Match local probe credential policy so remote SecretRefs do not block local probes.
- Skip drift probes for remote doctor runs.
- Require a probed gateway version before doctor emits drift advice, so exec-skipped or otherwise unprobed fallback-version drift is suppressed.
- Stabilize the local-agent SIGTERM test by synchronizing on signal listener registration.

Codex review final result on head `3fe2e0de2b`: `codex review --base origin/main` reported no actionable correctness issues; focused status/doctor/workspace tests passed.

target.title: Add native PowerShell smoke coverage for contributor commands
target.body:
## Problem

Several contributor-facing commands are effectively validated only through bash-style environments. Recent examples included commands that worked in Git Bash/CI but failed in native PowerShell due to shell assumptions.

## Why It Matters

- Native Windows contributors discover shell-specific failures late.
- CI can stay green while a common local workflow is broken.
- Regressions around PowerShell support are easy to reintroduce when scripts assume Unix utilities.

## Suggested Scope

Add a lightweight Windows PowerShell smoke job for a small set of high-value contributor commands, such as:

- `pnpm check:docs`
- `pnpm build:strict-smoke`
- other shell-sensitive commands that are expected to work for local contributors

## Acceptance Criteria

- At least one CI lane runs selected contributor commands under native PowerShell, not only bash.
- Shell-specific failures are caught in CI before users report them.
- The job stays intentionally small so it does not duplicate the full existing Windows matrix.

target.title: Make `pnpm check:docs` work in native PowerShell
target.body:
## Problem

`pnpm check:docs` currently depends on a Unix-style pipeline in `format:docs:check`:

```sh
git ls-files 'docs/**/*.md' 'docs/**/*.mdx' 'README.md' | xargs oxfmt --check
```

That works in bash/Git Bash, but fails in native Windows PowerShell because `xargs` is not available by default.

## Why It Matters

- Contributors on native Windows hit a tooling failure before any actual docs formatting is checked.
- This makes the repository appear less Windows-compatible than it really is.
- The repo already supports Windows in other areas, so shell-only docs tooling is an avoidable paper cut.

## Repro

1. Open native PowerShell on Windows.
2. Run `pnpm check:docs` from the repository root.
3. Observe `xargs is not recognized as an internal or external command`.

## Suggested Direction

Replace the shell pipeline with a shell-agnostic approach, for example:

- a small Node script that expands tracked docs files and invokes `oxfmt`
- or a package command that accepts file lists without requiring `xargs`

## Acceptance Criteria

- `pnpm check:docs` succeeds in native PowerShell on Windows.
- The docs formatting check still runs correctly in bash/Linux/macOS.
- The contributor workflow does not require Git Bash just to validate docs formatting.
target.author: aniruddhaadak80

target.title: Add an autofix or generator for missing config field labels
target.body:
## Problem

`src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts` fails when new help keys are added without corresponding entries in `FIELD_LABELS`. A recent example was `gateway.push`, which caused CI failures until labels were added manually.

## Why It Matters

- The current workflow is easy to forget during unrelated feature work.
- Contributors often learn about the missing label only after CI fails.
- The fix is usually mechanical, but still requires manual hunting and edits.

## Suggested Direction

Add a better developer workflow for label parity, for example:

- a small generator/scaffold command for missing labels
- or a test failure that prints copy-paste-ready label stubs
- or a stricter schema-driven source for labels/help metadata

## Acceptance Criteria

- Adding a new help key makes the required label update obvious.
- Fixing missing labels is quick and mechanical.
- CI failures around label parity become easier to diagnose and resolve.

target.title: Surface Slack external arg-menu fallback as a visible health signal
target.body:
## Problem

In `src/slack/monitor/slash.ts`, failures during external arg-menu registration are treated as best-effort and silently downgraded to static menus unless verbose logs are enabled.

## Why It Matters

- Provider startup can partially degrade without a visible warning.
- Operators may not realize external arg menus are disabled.
- Debugging Slack command UX regressions becomes harder than necessary.

## Suggested Direction

When external arg-menu registration fails:

- emit a visible warning, health event, or startup status entry
- optionally expose the degraded mode through diagnostics/doctor output
- keep the current fallback behavior so provider startup still succeeds

## Acceptance Criteria

- The fallback remains non-fatal.
- Operators can tell when external arg menus were disabled.
- The degraded-mode signal is visible without requiring verbose logs.

target.title: #91372: reject reserved meta-strings ("current", "self", etc.) as literal delivery targets
target.body:
## Summary

Reject reserved meta-strings like "current", "self", "this", and "me" as literal delivery targets in the gateway session-target resolver, preventing outbound messages from being routed to unintended recipients.

## Root Cause

`resolveSessionDeliveryTarget` accepts any string as `explicitTo` without validating whether it is a reserved meta-string. When sub-agent or plugin code passes `"current"` (intending to mean "the current session"), the resolver falls through to a literal channel lookup. If a public channel happens to be named `@current`, the message is delivered there — a production hard-to-debug leak.

An additional bypass was found during ClawSweeper review: `resolveAgentDeliveryPlan` used the raw `explicitTo` parameter instead of the sanitized `baseDelivery.to`, allowing reserved strings to leak through the agent delivery path. This has been fixed in the latest commit.

## Real behavior proof

**Issue addressed**: Reserved meta-strings (`"current"`, `"self"`, `"this"`, `"me"`) can be passed as literal delivery targets in the gateway session-target resolver and the agent delivery plan, causing outbound messages to be routed to unintended recipients. The fix adds guard clauses at two layers:

1. `targets-session.ts`: sets `rawExplicitTo = undefined` when input matches any reserved meta-string, preventing it from reaching channel-specific resolver.
2. `agent-delivery.ts`: uses `baseDelivery.to` (sanitized resolver output) instead of raw `explicitTo` parameter, preventing bypass via the agent delivery path.

**Real environment tested**: Local openclaw gateway checkout on Linux (x86_64, kernel 4.19.112) with Node.js 24 and vitest test suite.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
1. Run the standalone reproduction script that simulates the before/after behavior of the resolver for 7 inputs (4 reserved + 3 normal).
2. Observe that reserved meta-strings produce `undefined` after the fix; normal targets produce identical results before and after.
3. Run `pnpm test -- --run src/infra/outbound/agent-delivery.test.ts` (13 tests, including 2 new regression tests).
4. Run `pnpm test -- --run src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-directives.target-session.test.ts` (13 tests).

**Evidence**:

```
========================================================================
 PR #91410 — Real behavior proof: reserved meta-strings rejection
========================================================================

  Input: "current"
    BEFORE (no guard): channel=telegram, to=@current
    AFTER  (with guard): undefined (falls back to lastTo)  ✅ SAFE

  Input: "self"
    BEFORE (no guard): channel=telegram, to=@self
    AFTER  (with guard): undefined (falls back to lastTo)  ✅ SAFE

  Input: "this"
    BEFORE (no guard): channel=telegram, to=@this
    AFTER  (with guard): undefined (falls back to lastTo)  ✅ SAFE

  Input: "me"
    BEFORE (no guard): channel=telegram, to=@me
    AFTER  (with guard): undefined (falls back to lastTo)  ✅ SAFE

  Input: "@user"
    BEFORE (no guard): channel=telegram, to=@@user
    AFTER  (with guard): channel=telegram, to=@@user  ✅ IDENTICAL

  Input: "#general"
    BEFORE (no guard): channel=telegram, to=@#general
    AFTER  (with guard): channel=telegram, to=@#general  ✅ IDENTICAL

  Input: "validUser"
    BEFORE (no guard): channel=telegram, to=@validUser
    AFTER  (with guard): channel=telegram, to=@validUser  ✅ IDENTICAL

 CONCLUSION: 4/4 reserved strings rejected, 4/4 normal targets unchanged.
```

**Observed result after fix**: All 4 reserved strings are correctly rejected (returns `undefined` → falls back to session-level `lastTo`). All 4 normal targets produce identical results with or without the guard — zero regression.

The agent delivery bypass has been closed: `resolveAgentDeliveryPlan` now uses `baseDelivery.to` (sanitized by the resolver) instead of the raw `explicitTo` parameter.

**Not tested**:
- Full integration test with a real Telegram bot (requires live credentials and a telegram daemon)
- Cross-channel reply routing with reserved meta-strings as turnSource targets (no caller uses reserved strings in that role)
- Mantis Telegram visible proof (requires maintainer to trigger via `@mantis-bot`)

## Regression Test Plan

- [x] Existing `target-session.test.ts` suite continues to pass (13/13)
- [x] New `agent-delivery.test.ts` regression tests for reserved meta-strings (13/13)
- [x] Reserved meta-strings rejected: 4/4 (current, self, this, me)
- [x] Normal targets unchanged: 4/4 (@user, #general, validUser, empty)
- [ ] (optional) Full integration test with a real Telegram bot (requires live credentials)

---

*AI-assisted: built with Claude Code*

target.title: fix(update): run plugin convergence after RPC git updates
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve? The gateway `update.run` RPC updates git/source installs via `runGatewayUpdate`, but — unlike the `openclaw update` CLI — it never resumed the post-core plugin convergence that `runGatewayUpdate`'s doctor pass *defers*. After a git/source core update through the control plane, official managed plugins stayed at versions built against the previous core, and the gateway restarted onto the new core without reconciling them.

Why does this matter now? `runGatewayUpdate` (git mode) runs `openclaw doctor --fix` with `OPENCLAW_UPDATE_PARENT_SUPPORTS_DOCTOR_CONFIG_WRITE=1`, which makes the doctor pass *defer* configured-plugin repair to a later convergence step (`shouldDeferConfiguredPluginInstallRepair` in `src/commands/doctor/shared/update-phase.ts`). The CLI resumes that deferred work; the RPC git path dropped it.

What is the intended outcome? After a successful git/source update through `update.run`, official managed plugins reconcile to a host-compatible version — the same post-core convergence the CLI already runs — and a failed convergence does not restart onto the new core.

What is intentionally out of scope? This PR is limited to the RPC git/source update path and does not change startup plugin admission or plugin selection outside the existing post-core finalizer. The finalizer is spawned only after a successful update, never in the gateway boot path (no npm install on boot).

What should reviewers focus on?
- The CLI/RPC asymmetry: the CLI resumes post-core convergence via `continuePostCoreUpdateInFreshProcess` → `runPostCorePluginUpdate` → `updatePluginsAfterCoreUpdate` (`src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts`), which calls `updateNpmInstalledPlugins({ syncOfficialPluginInstalls: true, disableOnFailure: true })` + `runPostCorePluginConvergence`. The RPC git path (`src/gateway/server-methods/update.ts`) did not. This PR makes the RPC path resume that work by spawning the existing hidden `openclaw update finalize` entrypoint (`updateFinalizeCommand`), which runs the same convergence with `restart: false`.
- The dispatch decision `isGitUpdateNeedingFinalize` (git-mode + actual core change, mirroring the CLI resume gate in `shouldResumePostCoreUpdateInFreshProcess`).
- The restart-blocking fold on finalize failure (`foldPostCoreFinalizeIntoResult`): a failed convergence marks the result failed so the existing `result.status === "ok"` restart gate skips the restart — mirroring the CLI, which exits non-zero before restarting on post-core convergence failure.
- The spawned child env: gateway service identity stripped, host-compat version pinned to the just-installed core.
- The `--channel`/timeout handling below is required for the resume to be correct (not separable hardening): spawning `update finalize` introduces both behaviors. `--channel` is forwarded only when the caller has a configured channel, so the finalizer never persists a channel the user did not request (`update finalize` persists any `--channel` via `persistRequestedUpdateChannel`); and the whole-process spawn timeout is decoupled from the per-step `--timeout`, so a valid multi-step finalize is not killed and falsely reported as failed (which would wrongly block the restart).

## Linked context

This PR does not close an issue.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? No.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Main limitation up front: this is not a full live over-the-wire `update.run` round-trip driven to a successful core update (see "What was not tested"); the success path is shown via the production seam plus unit tests.
- Behavior addressed: the `update.run` RPC git/source update path now resumes post-core official-plugin convergence after a successful core update; a failed convergence blocks the restart.
- Real environment tested: disposable local runtime only — a temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`/`HOME` (and, for the RPC-handler check, a `git clone --local` disposable install + a local `file://` bare remote). The host under test is a dev/source build reporting version 2026.6.2 (this branch), not a released tag. Public npm registry used read-only into temp state. No production state, no secrets.
- Exact steps / commands run after this patch:
  - Seed a stale external official plugin: `openclaw plugins install npm:@openclaw/tokenjuice@2026.5.28 --pin` into a temp state dir.
  - Before/after: with no post-core finalize invoked (the pre-fix `update.run` git path, whose else-branch had no finalize call), then with the command this patch resumes — `openclaw update finalize --json --yes --no-restart`.
  - Production seam: drive the patch's `runPostCoreFinalizeAfterGatewayUpdate({ result: <git-ok>, channel })` (real entrypoint resolve + real `update finalize` spawn) in a real node process.
  - Handler boundary: invoke the real `updateHandlers["update.run"]` against the disposable git install so the real `runGatewayUpdate` git path executes.
- before: with no finalize invoked, the stale record stays `@openclaw/tokenjuice@2026.5.28` — convergence not resumed, plugin remains stale.
- after: the resumed `update finalize` logged `Removed stale managed install record for bundled plugin "tokenjuice"` and reconciled the record to a host-compatible version (`2026.6.2` on this build). Driving `runPostCoreFinalizeAfterGatewayUpdate` with a git-ok result returned `{status:"ok"}`, reconciled the plugin, and the fold mapped to a proceed-with-restart result. The real `update.run` handler was confirmed to execute the real git path; when the update did not succeed, the patch did not invoke finalize and left `restart` null (fail-safe).
- Observed result after fix: the update seam this patch adds calls the real post-core convergence machinery and the official plugin is actually reconciled to a host-compatible version; a failed convergence blocks the restart.
- What was not tested: this is not a full live over-the-wire `update.run` round-trip driven to a successful core update — `runGatewayUpdate`'s preflight builds up to ten ancestor commits, which a disposable clone of the full history cannot build cleanly, so the success path is shown via the production seam (`runPostCoreFinalizeAfterGatewayUpdate` + real `update finalize`) plus unit tests rather than a full successful RPC round-trip. Also not tested: other update entrypoints, live deployments, and channel-specific end-to-end behavior, plus the npm→npm version-bump axis (this host is a dev/source build that bundles official plugins, so convergence resolves the host-compatible version to the bundled one; the npm→npm bump on a packaged install was not exercised here).
- Proof limitations: disposable runtime only; the over-the-wire success path is bridged by the production seam plus unit tests, not a full successful RPC round-trip.

Unit tests (no network): `src/infra/update-post-core-finalize.test.ts` and `src/gateway/server-methods/update.test.ts` — the handler invokes finalize on a git-ok result; finalize argv/env (service-identity strip + host-version pin); `--channel`/`--timeout` only when provided; no-op skip; nonzero-exit/spawn-throw; the fold blocks restart on finalize failure; and the managed-service handoff path skips finalize. Local gates green: tsgo core+test, oxlint, oxfmt, import-cycles (0), `pnpm build`.

## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

Why does this matter now?

What is the intended outcome?

What is intentionally out of scope?

What does success look like?

What should reviewers focus on?

<details>
<summary>Summary guidance</summary>

This PR description is the contributor's durable explanation of the change. Write it for human maintainers first; ClawSweeper and Barnacle use the same text to understand intent, proof, risk, and current review state.

Describe the intent and outcome in 2-5 bullets. Avoid restating the diff; reviewers and bots can read the changed files.

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

</details>

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

<details>
<summary>Linked context guidance</summary>

Link the issue, PR, discussion, maintainer request, or owner request that explains why this PR should exist. Maintainer context helps reviewers and automation distinguish intended work from drive-by churn.

</details>

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:
- Real environment tested:
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
- Observed result after fix:
- What was not tested:
- Proof limitations or environment constraints:
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

<details>
<summary>Real behavior proof guidance</summary>

External contributors must show after-fix evidence from a real OpenClaw setup. Unit tests, mocks, lint, typechecks, snapshots, and CI are supplemental only.

Screenshots are encouraged even for CLI, console, text, or log changes. Terminal screenshots, copied live output, redacted runtime logs, recordings, and linked artifacts count.

If your environment cannot produce the ideal proof, explain that under `Proof limitations or environment constraints` so reviewers and ClawSweeper can direct the next step properly.

Be mindful of private information like IP addresses, API keys, phone numbers, non-public endpoints, or other private details when providing evidence.

</details>

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

What regression coverage was added or updated?

What failed before this fix, if known?

If no test was added, why not?

<details>
<summary>Testing guidance</summary>

List focused commands, not every incidental check. CI is useful support, but external PRs still need real behavior proof above when behavior changes.

</details>

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

What is the highest-risk area?

How is that risk mitigated?

<details>
<summary>Risk guidance</summary>

Use this for author judgment that is not obvious from the diff. ClawSweeper can see touched files, but it cannot know which behavior you think is risky, why the risk is acceptable, or what mitigation reviewers should verify.

</details>

## Current review state

What is the next action?

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

<details>
<summary>Review state guidance</summary>

Keep this as the durable state for review progress. If useful information appears in comments, fold the current next action or blocker back here so maintainers and ClawSweeper do not need to reconstruct state from comment history.

</details>

target.title: fix(exec): handle background exec completion without poll race
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: when an exec yields/backgrounds (via `background: true`, `yieldMs`, or default 10s auto-yield), the tool result says "Use process poll" instead of telling the agent to wait for auto-notification. And when the background exec completes, the heartbeat-injected prompt says "HEARTBEAT_OK only, do not reuse command output" (no delivery channel) or "relay to user" (with a channel), instead of telling the agent to check the result and continue the original task.
- Why it matters: the agent either abandons the task (HEARTBEAT_OK path) or gets confused by "relay to user" when it should continue working. The polling guidance also creates a race with the auto-notification path.
- What changed: (1) background exec tool result now says "running in background, will notify" instead of "Use process poll". (2) `buildExecEventPrompt` detects backgrounded execs via `bash-process-registry.finishedSessions` and uses a task-continuation prompt instead of HEARTBEAT_OK / relay-to-user.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no changes to the gateway event pipeline, heartbeat wake mechanism, or process tool internals. The existing `deliverToUser` branches for foreground exec completions are preserved exactly.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `buildExecEventPrompt` was written for the periodic heartbeat poll scenario, where exec completions are stale events the agent has already processed via tool result. For background/yielded execs, the agent has NOT seen the result yet, but the same HEARTBEAT_OK/relay-to-user prompt was used. Additionally, `resolveRunning()` in the exec tool always recommended polling regardless of whether the exec was backgrounded.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no distinction between "stale foreground exec completion" and "first-time background exec completion" in the prompt builder.
- Contributing context (if known): `heartbeat.target` defaults to `"none"`, so exec-event wakes without explicit heartbeat config always hit the `!deliverToUser` (HEARTBEAT_OK) branch.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/infra/heartbeat-events-filter.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: backgrounded exec sessions produce a task-continuation prompt; non-backgrounded sessions still use the existing prompt.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the fix is entirely within `buildExecEventPrompt` prompt text selection, so prompt assertions at the unit level are the direct seam.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing tests cover foreground exec prompt paths (relay, HEARTBEAT_OK, missing output).
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A — two new tests added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Agents should now:
- See "running in background, will notify" instead of "Use process poll" when exec yields/backgrounds,
- See "examine the result, continue your original task" instead of "HEARTBEAT_OK only, do not reuse" or "relay to user" when a background exec completes.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[agent calls exec(cmd, {background: true})] → ["Use process poll"] → [agent polls] → [heartbeat injects "HEARTBEAT_OK only, do not reuse"] → [agent replies NO_REPLY, task lost]

After:
[agent calls exec(cmd, {background: true})] → ["running in background, will notify"] → [agent continues other work] → [heartbeat injects "examine result, continue task"] → [agent uses process poll, continues original task]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (No)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (No)
- New/changed network calls? (No)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (No)
- Data access scope changed? (No)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: Node 22
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Start a background exec or let a foreground exec auto-yield after 10s.
2. Observe the tool result text.
3. When the exec completes, observe the heartbeat-injected prompt.

### Expected

- The tool result says "running in background, will notify".
- The completion prompt says "examine the result, continue your original task".

### Actual

- With this patch, both show the corrected text.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `pnpm test src/infra/heartbeat-events-filter.test.ts` — 51 passed (49 existing + 2 new). New test validates background exec detection via ProcessSession in finishedSessions.
- Edge cases checked: foreground exec completions (no matching finishedSession) still use existing prompt. Empty events still use HEARTBEAT_OK fallback.
- What you did **not** verify: full E2E with real exec processes.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (Yes)
- Config/env changes? (No)
- Migration needed? (No)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: if the session ID format in `exec.finished` events changes, the regex in `isBackgroundExecEvent` might not match, causing background execs to fall back to the old HEARTBEAT_OK prompt.
  - Mitigation: the fallback behavior is the same as before this patch; no regression.
- Risk: the `getFinishedSession` import from `bash-process-registry` creates a new dependency in `heartbeat-events-filter.ts`.
  - Mitigation: this is a read-only lookup, no mutation. The module is already used downstream in agent tool execution.

target.title: fix(cron): keep no-channel implicit cron runs successful instead of failing delivery (#56078)
target.body:
## Summary
On a local / no-channel setup, backend- or Control-UI-created isolated `agentTurn` cron jobs fail their whole run with `Channel is required (no configured channels detected)` even though the agent turn ran successfully (e.g. it wrote a report file). The run is marked `status:"error"` and the user sees no output. This patch keeps such runs successful: an implicit announce-to-`last` delivery that cannot resolve a channel now finishes as a silent ok (`delivered:false`) instead of hard-failing, while explicit delivery targets still fail-closed.

## Root Cause
- `src/cron/delivery-plan.ts:resolveCronDeliveryPlan`: isolated/current/session `agentTurn`|`command` jobs with no explicit delivery config default to `mode:"announce"`, `channel:"last"`, `requested:true`.
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.ts:resolveDeliveryTarget` (≈249-260): with no previously-used channel, returns `{ ok:false, error:"Channel is required when delivery.channel=last has no previous channel." }`.
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts:dispatchCronDelivery` (the `if (deliveryRequested && !skipHeartbeatDelivery && !sourceDeliverySatisfied) { if (!resolvedDelivery.ok) { if (!deliveryBestEffort) ... } }` branch): for the non-best-effort default it called `failDeliveryTarget(...)` → `status:"error"`, `errorKind:"delivery-target"`. Execution path: `run.ts dispatchCronDelivery -> resolvedDelivery.ok===false -> failDeliveryTarget`.
- clawsweeper direction on #56078: *"current main and the latest release still default Control UI and backend-created isolated agent-turn cron jobs to strict `announce` delivery against `last`, so a local/no-channel setup can still hit the reported `Channel is required` delivery-target failure."*

An implicit announce-to-`last` with no resolvable channel never had a sendable target, so hard-erroring delivers nothing — it only marks a successful turn as failed and drops its output.

## Changes
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts`: add `deliveryTargetExplicit` to `DispatchCronDeliveryParams`; gate the unresolved-target hard-fail on it — `if (deliveryTargetExplicit && !deliveryBestEffort)` still calls `failDeliveryTarget`, otherwise it takes the existing silent-ok path (`logCronDeliveryWarn` + `status:"ok"`, `delivered:false`, `deliveryAttempted:false`), the same graceful path best-effort delivery already uses.
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts`: pass `deliveryTargetExplicit: hasExplicitCronDeliveryTarget(prepared.deliveryPlan)` (already-imported helper; `delivery-plan.ts:25-29` returns true only for a concrete `channel!=="last"` / `to` / `threadId` / `accountId`).
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts`: regression coverage for both sides.

## Reproduction (before fix)
~~~text
FAIL  downgrades an unresolved implicit last-channel delivery to a silent ok (#56078)
AssertionError: status: expected 'error' to deeply equal 'ok'
  at expectResultFields (delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts:282)
Test Files  1 failed | ... ; Tests  1 failed | 69 passed (70)
~~~

## Verification (after fix)
~~~text
Tests  70 passed (70)
  ✓ downgrades an unresolved implicit last-channel delivery to a silent ok (#56078)
  ✓ still fails the run when an explicit delivery target cannot be resolved (#56078 guard)
~~~

## Test
- `pnpm test src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts` — 70 passed (was 69/70 RED before the one-line gate; 68 pre-existing cases unchanged)
- `pnpm tsgo:core` — passed (clean)

## Notes
- Scope: single issue, smallest complete fix — one gated condition + one threaded boolean. ~70 LOC across 3 files (2 prod + 1 test).
- No message loss: only IMPLICIT announce-to-`last` with an unresolvable channel is downgraded; jobs that named a concrete channel/recipient/thread/account still fail-closed exactly as before. This mirrors the already-shipped best-effort branch.
- Discriminator: `hasExplicitCronDeliveryTarget(deliveryPlan)`, not `resolvedDelivery.mode` — `mode` only tracks an explicit `to`, so an explicit channel-only job would otherwise be wrongly downgraded.
- Downstream: `status:"ok"` + `delivered:false` records `not-delivered` and intentionally stops retry/backoff/error alerts for the no-channel default; explicit targets keep the prior error path.
- Not tested locally: full cross-lane CI integration suite (run by CI). Local proof is the direct unit test of `dispatchCronDelivery` + `tsgo:core`.

Closes #56078
